title: erigeron quercifolius
intro: Erigeron quercifolius, commonly known as oak-leaved fleabane, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions. Erigeron quercifolius can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional folk medicine.
